# JA4 / JA4S Threat-Hunting Workbook

Surfaces the most interesting TLS-fingerprint activity in this workspace and explains **why** each item is interesting. Runs on Defender data streamed here (`DeviceNetworkEvents` SslConnectionInspected, `SecurityIncident`/`SecurityAlert`, `ConnectionSuccess` process data).

**How items are scored**
- **Rarity** = combined: rare only if on **few hosts AND few connections**. The **Min rarity** slider focuses every section on the rare tail.
- **Incident-match fidelity** = proximity (flow-exact > host+time > host-any > shared-IP) x temporal decay x rarity.
- **Malice signals** = JA4 structure (no-SNI / no-ALPN / legacy TLS), process attribution (LOLBIN / user-path), cert anomalies (self-signed / IP-SNI / free CA).
- **Beaconing** = low-jitter periodic call-homes.

Pick a **Lookback** window and a **Min rarity** focus below (default *Rare* = few hosts AND few connections), then start at **Section 2 (Top Prioritized Leads)**.

## 1. Summary
At-a-glance counts. **Rare pairs** and **self-signed certs** are where to start.

## 2. Top Prioritized Leads - what's interesting and why
The highest-signal items across all engines, ranked. **Why** explains each: a suspicious fingerprint, a fingerprint tied to an incident's device/IP, or a periodic call-home. Work the top (red) rows first.

## 3. Suspicious rare JA4 - triage
Every JA4 x JA4S pair scored for maliciousness. **Verdict / Reasons** say *why* (LOLBIN process, self-signed cert, no-SNI...). **Procs** is the process behind the fingerprint (recovered via ConnectionSuccess).

## 4. Incident-match fidelity
Which fingerprints are tied to your incidents and how tightly. **MatchTier** = flow-exact > host+time > host-any > shared-IP; **MatchFidelity** decays by time-gap and is weighted by rarity. **DrivingIncident** is the incident it matched.

## 5. Beaconing call-homes
Regular, low-jitter TLS to one destination = classic C2 shape. A **rare** JA4 to a **no-SNI / raw-IP** destination with low **JitterCV** is the top lead. Benign telemetry (Microsoft, AV, browsers) also beacons - triage by **Dest**.

## 6. JA4 rarity landscape
Fingerprints by **combined rarity** (0 = ubiquitous, 1 = singleton on both hosts and connections). The **right-hand bars are the rare tail** - your hunting ground; the **Min rarity** slider focuses every other section there.

### Methodology & sources
MISP decaying models (temporal decay) | Pyramid of Pain (JA4 ranks above IP) | JA4 spec (structure decode) | detection-engineering proximity scoring | Recorded Future / STIX confidence bands. All signals are Microsoft E5 / Defender-native - no external threat-intel feeds.
